title: using unpacked array as a module port (and module parameter)
body:
Using Git version of Verilator.

I will try to describe various errors I encountered, but will not provide code to reproduce each one, since they are probably related. At the end are instructions to reproduce one error.

https://github.com/jeras/rp32/blob/b632d8139262eaa1a9f8ccd090921de89ae6b30e/rtl/r5p_bus_dec.sv
The decoder is also using *binary wildcard equality operator* `==?` operator and *array reduction method* `and()`.
I first assumed they are responsible for some issues, but I still have issues with parameter array without this extra constructs.

### Array as parameter

I defined an array parameter inside `#()` of a module.
```SystemVerilog
parameter logic [AW-1:0] AS [BN-1:0] = '{BN{'x}}
```
I tried to instantiate the module with the next configuration.
```SystemVerilog
  .AS  ('{DAW'('h0_xxxx),   // 0x0_0000 ~ 0x0_ffff - data memory
          DAW'('h1_xxxx)})  // 0x1_0000 ~ 0x1_ffff - controller
```
I get the next error:
```
%Error-UNSUPPORTED: ../tbn/r5p_tb.sv:114:9: Unsupported/Illegal: Assignment pattern member not underneath a supported construct: PIN 'AS'
                                          : ... In instance r5p_tb
  114 |   .AS  ('{DAW'('h0_xxxx),    
      |         ^~
                    ... For error description see https://verilator.org/warn/UNSUPPORTED?v=4.202
%Error: ../tbn/r5p_tb.sv:114:9: Expecting expression to be constant, but can't convert a PATTERN to constant.
                              : ... In instance r5p_tb
  114 |   .AS  ('{DAW'('h0_xxxx),    
      |         ^~
%Error: ../tbn/r5p_tb.sv:114:4: Can't convert defparam value to constant: Param 'AS' of 'ls_dec'
                              : ... In instance r5p_tb
  114 |   .AS  ('{DAW'('h0_xxxx),    
      |    ^~
%Warning-WIDTH: ../rtl/r5p_bus_dec.sv:12:18: Operator VAR 'AS' expects 17 bits on the Initial value, but Initial value's CONST '?1?h0' generates 1 bits.
                                           : ... In instance r5p_tb.ls_dec
   12 |   logic [AW-1:0] AS [BN-1:0] = '{BN{'x}}
      |                  ^~
                ... Use "/* verilator lint_off WIDTH */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
```

### Using parameter array in generate loop

I cant reproduce this one right now, so I will just write down what I remember.
Verilator complained about the expression `AS[i]` not being a constant, but is should be, as `AS` is a parameter.

```
generate
for (genvar i=0; i<BN; i++) begin
  assign s_dec[i] = s_adr ==? AS[i];
end
endgenerate
```

### Compilation error

I get an error while compiling the generated C code.
```
Vr5p_tb.cpp: In static member function ‘static void Vr5p_tb::_sequent__TOP__33(Vr5p_tb__Syms*)’:
Vr5p_tb.cpp:918:37: error: no match for ‘operator&’ (operand types are ‘IData’ {aka ‘unsigned int’} and ‘VlUnpacked<unsigned char, 2>’)
  917 |                             [0U] = ((IData)(vlTOPp->r5p_tb__DOT__ls_dec__DOT__m_dec)
      |                                     ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
      |                                     |
      |                                     IData {aka unsigned int}
  918 |                                     & vlTOPp->r5p_tb__DOT____Vcellinp__ls_dec__m_ack);
      |                                     ^ ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
      |                                               |
      |                                               VlUnpacked<unsigned char, 2>
In file included from /opt/systemc-2.3.2/include/sysc/communication/sc_port.h:35,
                 from /opt/systemc-2.3.2/include/sysc/communication/sc_signal.h:32,
                 from /opt/systemc-2.3.2/include/sysc/communication/sc_buffer.h:34,
                 from /opt/systemc-2.3.2/include/systemc:79,
                 from /opt/systemc-2.3.2/include/systemc.h:219,
                 from Vr5p_tb.h:11,
                 from Vr5p_tb.cpp:5:
/opt/systemc-2.3.2/include/sysc/kernel/sc_event.h:817:1: note: candidate: ‘sc_core::sc_event_and_expr sc_core::operator&(sc_core::sc_event_and_expr, const sc_core::sc_event&)’
  817 | operator & ( sc_event_and_expr expr, sc_event const & e )
      | ^~~~~~~~
/opt/systemc-2.3.2/include/sysc/kernel/sc_event.h:817:32: note:   no known conversion for argument 1 from ‘IData’ {aka ‘unsigned int’} to ‘sc_core::sc_event_and_expr’ {aka ‘sc_core::sc_event_expr<sc_core::sc_event_and_list>’}
  817 | operator & ( sc_event_and_expr expr, sc_event const & e )
      |              ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^~~~
/opt/systemc-2.3.2/include/sysc/kernel/sc_event.h:825:1: note: candidate: ‘sc_core::sc_event_and_expr sc_core::operator&(sc_core::sc_event_and_expr, const sc_core::sc_event_and_list&)’
  825 | operator & ( sc_event_and_expr expr, sc_event_and_list const & el )
      | ^~~~~~~~
/opt/systemc-2.3.2/include/sysc/kernel/sc_event.h:825:32: note:   no known conversion for argument 1 from ‘IData’ {aka ‘unsigned int’} to ‘sc_core::sc_event_and_expr’ {aka ‘sc_core::sc_event_expr<sc_core::sc_event_and_list>’}
  825 | operator & ( sc_event_and_expr expr, sc_event_and_list const & el )
      |              ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^~~~
Vr5p_tb.cpp:921:45: error: no match for ‘operator&’ (operand types are ‘IData’ {aka ‘unsigned int’} and ‘VlUnpacked<unsigned char, 2>’)
  920 |                             [1U] = (((IData)(vlTOPp->r5p_tb__DOT__ls_dec__DOT__m_dec)
      |                                     ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
  921 |                                      >> 1U) & vlTOPp->r5p_tb__DOT____Vcellinp__ls_dec__m_ack);
      |                                      ~~~~~~ ^ ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
      |                                      |                |
      |                                      |                VlUnpacked<unsigned char, 2>
      |                                      IData {aka unsigned int}
```

To reproduce:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/jeras/rp32.git -b b632d81
cd rp32
cd sim
make -f Makefile.verilator
```
